note: Agent control-plane config for this project — the 5-layer governance stack (memory → skills → hooks → subagents → plugins). Config-as-code; distributed via plugins for whole-team parity.

layers.name: Memory
layers.value: Unify naming, structure and repo rules so every agent aligns to the same baseline.
layers.role: Global config, project rules, code standards, engineering red-lines — written-in-stone team consensus.

layers.name: Skills
layers.value: Distil best practices so agents match the right capability to the task — no reinventing.
layers.role: On-demand workflows the agent auto-recognises and invokes only when relevant.

layers.name: Guardrails
layers.value: Block risk before it happens, keep an after-the-fact record — safe, controllable agents.
layers.role: Deterministic pre/post lifecycle enforcement: block dangerous commands, auto-deploy notices, permission gates.
items: ZERO-API guard — grading never triggers the AI grader

layers.name: Subagents
layers.value: Each subagent gets its own context window, tools and permissions; results return to the main thread, keeping it clean.
layers.role: Isolated context, parallel execution, no cross-talk — raises throughput and quality.

layers.name: Plugins
layers.value: NPM-style distribution with version control — one-click install syncs the whole team's config.
layers.role: Bundle skills + hooks + subagents; one install keeps every teammate's setup identical.
